episodes.title: Signs Your Cult-ure Drifted Into Cult-like, with Candice Schutter
episodes.description: Emotional intelligence is supposed to make workplaces better. But what if it might also teach people to tolerate what should be intolerable? Candice Schutter spent two decades inside the wellness industry, leading programs, training instructors, building cultures of care, until her body started screaming that something was wrong through panic attacks and a nervous system in overdrive. She started digging into cult dynamics, coercive control, and the psychology of mission-driven organizations. What she found changed everything. In this conversation with Dr. Mira Brancu, Candice breaks down what she calls "the drift," the slow, invisible process by which caring cultures slide into high-control environments. You will learn when wellness programs can train people to tolerate dysfunction, why high emotional intelligence can suggest greater ability to manipulate, and what the dark side of transformational leadership looks like from the inside. If you have ever felt a disconnect between the language of psychological safety and the reality of structural control at your workplace, this episode will give you the frameworks to name what you have been feeling. Subscribe for weekly conversations on leadership, mental health, and navigating workplace dynamics.
